content:
“The goal of the public transparency is for the public to judge the success of some group of experts. To serve that function, the metric has to be comprehensible by the public.” Following BJ Fogg’s model, the metric designers are anticipating a low LCD of ability and so the metric is getting deformed/bastardized on the process, losing its original intent. 

The Scot C Thi Nguyen p79

- Distill project into testable components (issues)
- Ephemeral observability per worktree (for debugging/tracing; tests do validation)
AGENTS.md file -> roadmap vs. detail
  - Subdirectories/files: ARCHITECTURE.md, DESIGN.md, FRONTEND.md, PLANS.md (how to write plans), PRODUCT_SENSE.md, QUALITY_SCORE.md, RELIABILITY.md, SECURITY.md, docs/ -> design-docs, exec-plans, generated, product-specs, references
- AGENTS.md (using symlink for worktree .env.local files, file size LOC...)
- ARCHITECTURE.md -> directional dependencies, centrally controleld boundaries with local autonomy ("You care deeply about boundaries, correctness, and reproducibility. Within those boundaries, you allow teams—or agents—significant freedom in how solutions are expressed.")
- human code review + other input act as documentation/evals to improve the system ("Review comments, refactoring pull requests, and user-facing bugs are captured as documentation updates or encoded directly into tooling. When documentation falls short, we promote the rule into code")

Different types of measures: principles, models, and algorithms. Recipes turning into algorithmic series of steps is the perfect example of this transition. America’s Test Kitchen is like that on steroids. athenahealth was an industry where it was backwards to start - people were treating medical billing like a principle measure, when it was an algo at heart. Extracting that knowledge is what led to their auccesss. 


The Score C Thi Nguyen p140

Good candidates for goals: performance optimization, flaky test investigation, dependency migrations, bug hunts that require reproduction, multi-step refactors, benchmark-driven tuning, and research tasks that require a final artifact.

One-off edit should be single prompt.

6 parts to good goal: Outcome, Verification Surface (e.g. test, benchmark, report, artifact - proof of goal), Constraints (regressions to avoid), Boundaries (files, tools, data, repos, etc. Codex can use), Iteration Policy (how to decide what to try next), Blocked Stop Condition (when it should cry uncle)

Template: /goal <desired end state> verified by <specific evidence> while preserving <constraints>. Use <allowed inputs, tools, or boundaries>. Between iterations, <how Codex should choose the next best action>. If blocked or no valid paths remain, <what Codex should report and what would unlock progress>.

Ex:
/goal Reduce p95 checkout latency below 120 ms without regressing correctness tests (passable but poor)
/goal Reduce p95 checkout latency below 120 ms, verified by the checkout benchmark, while keeping the correctness suite green. Use only the checkout service, benchmark fixtures, and related tests. Between iterations, record what changed, what the benchmark showed, and the next best experiment to try. If the benchmark cannot run or no valid paths remain, stop with the attempted paths, the evidence gathered, the blocker, and the next input needed. (full operating contract)

Example building a feature trying to match output of a paper:
/goal Produce the strongest evidence-backed reproduction of the paper using the available materials and local resources. Attempt the headline results where feasible, verify outputs where possible, and end with a report that separates confirmed findings, approximate reconstructions, blocked claims, and remaining uncertainty.

Games and joining a team, even for one game, is the best example of immediately taking on a new identity. Your best friend can end up on the other team and now you’re opponents. Great lesson for how valuable and powerful taking on a new identity can be, and how it can drive you motivations and level of intensity. Games ask you to take on a new identity within the game, and your motivations are shaped by the rules. 

The Score p13

Object art vs process art and process art -> “But other times, we design artifacts specifically to get us there, to encourage and sculpt beautiful action”. This could be translated to harness/context engineering and creating environment for creative coding process. This also makes me think of “the art of the woodworking jig” and making something that facilitates the process; makes the process more beautiful and smooth. 

The ascot C Thi Nguyen p95

AI inference costs for devs + GitHub copilot moving to straight usage billing vs subscription. AI just moved things along faster, but this follows the same VC-subsidized enshittification pattern of so many businesses. Reference Cory Doctorow and look into data about relative cost of an Uber ride compared to a taxi over time - see if it follows a pattern where the costs get closer to each other over time, but uber was cheaper earlier on due to VC subsidies.

Game designers are especially well equipped for the world of agentic development. It requires excellent understanding of how to apply guardrails (determinism) to inherently non-deterministic situations (humans doing pretty much anything) and controlling the outcomes to some extent. 

The Score C Thi Nguyen p112

Hobbes -> power is whoever has the right to control others actions, from ability to control language. Translating to product design -> agreeing on language up front removes the power struggle later on. 

From The Score p8 C Thi Nguyen

Learning values from community/family -> “you can start with an external value and tailor it to fit. But something very different happens when you’re value-captured by an institutional metric: You’re binding yourself to an inflexible external standard.” 

How is the community standard more flexible than the institutional? Can’t you still extricate yourself from the value source? There might something here about trying to distill things down to a binary, because it’s easier to understand things in black and white. It’s what they recommend in AI evals to get more signal from the feedback. 

The Score p49
